channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Air Macau's loss in 2025 reached the peak since the end of the pandemic
item.description:
Although operating revenues and the number of passengers registered slight increases, Air Macau's financial situation deteriorated again in 2025, with the carrier suffering net losses of almost 765 million patacas, which reflects an annual increase of 10% and the highest value after the end of the pandemic, according to data from Air China, the majority shareholder. Since 2020, the local transport company has always closed its accounts in the 'red', accumulating losses of around 4.5 billion patacas in six years
SERGIO TERRA
Air Macau recorded net losses of 655 million renminbis (around 764.5 million patacas at current exchange rates) in the 2025 financial year, reveals the annual report of Air China, the majority shareholder of the local carrier. This is the sixth consecutive result in the 'red', as well as the highest since the end of the pandemic, that is, since 2023, and represents a growth of 10.1% when compared to the losses of 595 million renminbis (694.5 million patacas) recorded in 2024, considering the values released by Air China, as the Macao SAR flag company ended up announcing losses of 677 million patacas. patacas that year, a discrepancy linked to accounting standards and exchange rate fluctuations. According to data compiled by the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper, between 2020 and 2025, Air Macau has already accumulated net losses of approximately 4.5 billion patacas, 2.6 times more than the total profits generated in the previous 10 years (around 1.7 billion). 2020 was the most negative year, with losses exceeding one billion patacas. The last positive financial year dates back to 2019, when net earnings amounted to more than 50.4 million patacas (29.3% less than in 2018), according to the annual report of the company's Board of Directors published in the Macao SAR Official Gazette. Despite the worsening of losses, operating revenues grew 2.6% to 3.19 billion renminbi (3.73 billion patacas) between 2024 and 2025. This improvement largely derived from the 2.1% increase in the number of passengers transported over the past year (around 3.28 million), which was equivalent to 89.6% of the record recorded in 2019 (3.66 million), while the flight occupancy rate increased by 1.45 percentage points to 76.05% year-on-year. On the other hand, the data included in the Air China report points to breaks in parameters that assess the relationship between supply and demand for air transport. Air Macau's RPK (average revenue per passenger/kilometer), which reflects real demand for transport, fell 1.29% compared to 2024 and ASK (available seats per kilometer) fell 3.17%. In 2025, Air Macau's fleet included 23 aircraft (the same number as the previous year), all manufactured by Airbus, with an average age of 9.14 years. At the end of 2025, Air Macau's total assets were valued at around 6.19 billion renminbi (7.23 billion patacas), an increase of 11.3% in annual variation. Due to the company's serious financial situation, on March 28, 2025, another operation to increase Air Macau's capital was completed, to 2.38 billion patacas, with China National Aviation Corporation (CNAC) – the state group that makes up Air China – now holding 74.94% of the total, assuming a reinforcement of eight percentage points, followed by Macao SAR, which now holds 24.08% (plus 2.58 points). The Sociedade de Turismo e Diversões de Macau (STDM) 'emptied' its weight as a shareholder, dropping from 11.57% to 0.97%, remaining just above the residual shares distributed by Evergreen Airways, IPE (Macau) - Investimentos e Participações Empresariais, Centro de Comércio Mundial Macau and Companhia de Seguros de Macau. In the operational field, Air Macau appears to face the coming months with renewed ambitions, particularly in the Southeast Asian market. The route plan for Summer/Autumn 2026 foresees the increase in the frequency of flights from Macau to Kuala Lumpur, Bangkok and Hanoi, and the reestablishment of connections to Manila, from 1 July, after almost two years of suspension. Connections to Fukuoka (Japan) should also resume in the summer, the carrier recently announced, which also plans to increase the frequency of flights to destinations in Mainland China (Wenzhou, Shanghai and Chengdu) and Taiwan (Kaohsiung and Taoyuan).
Air China's losses grew nearly eightfold Air China suffered net losses of around 1.77 billion renminbi (more than two billion patacas) in 2025, almost eight times more than in 2024, marking the sixth consecutive year with negative results. Since the beginning of this decade, Air China has accumulated losses of 72.71 billion renminbi. In 2025, operating income rose 2.87% to 171.48 billion renminbi, but expenses grew 3.11% to 177.14 billion. Last year, Air China carried 161 million passengers, 3.4% more than in 2024.
CAM profited 229 million The Sociedade do Aeroporto Internacional de Macau (CAM) had pre-tax profits of 229 million patacas in 2025, 59.8% less year-on-year, while revenues fell 8.7% to 1.35 billion. After the general meeting held on Friday, CAM also highlighted that, in response to the financing needs of the airport expansion project, shareholders subscribed to an increase of 530 million patacas in the company's capital in 2025. According to CAM, the repair and improvement works on the runway will begin “soon” and the cargo terminal project in Hengqin “is progressing in an orderly manner, with the structure expected to be completed in third quarter and from construction until the end of the year. In 2025, the Airport recorded 7.52 million passengers (1.6% less), 58 thousand flights (2.9% less) and 109 thousand tons of air cargo (1.1% more).

item.title: New consumption incentives launched between April and June
item.description:
The Government will launch a new round of the “Grand Prize for Consumption in Community Areas”. This time, however, the draw for benefits – which can be 10, 20, 50, 100 or 200 patacas – will take place between Friday and Sunday, with the coupons being able to be used between Monday and Thursday in more than 20 thousand stores. The idea is to encourage local consumption at the weekend and direct the benefits to different types of small and medium-sized companies. Elderly people and people with disabilities will have other benefits, namely a direct discount of 500 patacas and 50% discounts for goods or services worth 1 thousand patacas
CATARINA PEREIRA
The “Grand Prize for Consumption in Community Areas” is back, between April 10th and June 18th, that is, for 10 weeks. Once again, the objective is to “boost the consumption environment in community areas, stimulating confidence in the market”, and for the first time the draw will take place on the weekend, with coupons being able to be used on working days. Stressing that the previous editions were “widely well recognized by society”, the deputy director of the Economic and Technological Development Services (DSEDT), Cheang Hio Man, stressed that they contributed “effectively” to stimulating the local economy. This time, benefits worth a total of 400 million patacas will be distributed, continuing support measures for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) during the low tourist season, authorities added. “After widely listening to the opinions of society, the rules of the activity were duly adjusted, encouraging residents to stay and consume in Macau during the weekends to obtain benefits, thus changing the period for using the benefits to Monday to Thursday”, added Cheang Hio Man. He also called on traders to launch extra discounts, in order to boost the results of the initiative. According to him, in this way, periods of consumption can be dispersed, allowing the benefits to reach a greater number of different SMEs and increasing the global boosting effect. Therefore, unlike other editions, the draw will take place from Friday to Sunday, with coupons having to be used between Monday and Thursday. Residents who use an authenticated real-name electronic wallet, and who consume at eligible commercial establishments throughout the territory, paying 50 patacas in a single transaction, can draw three times to win benefits. With a nominal value of 10, 20, 50, 100 and 200 patacas, the benefits can then be used in more than 20 thousand physical stores, with the amount paid in each transaction being equal to or greater than triple the nominal value of the coupon. If you have several electronic benefits in the same electronic wallet, they will be automatically redeemed in a single transaction as long as the “triple coupon value” requirement is met. All discount coupons expire at midnight on the Friday following receipt. On the other hand, elderly people and holders of the Disability Assessment Registration Card will now obtain an additional immediate discount, worth 500 patacas, through the “Macau Pass Card for Disabled People”. Furthermore, the Government will allow, during the activity, seniors to purchase goods or services worth a total of 1,000 patacas with a 50% discount. In these cases, there is no daily limit for using the discount, and it can be used from Monday to Sunday during the activity period. Unused immediate discount benefits expire at the end of the activity and do not affect the value of the money itself. The activity is organized by the Government together with the Macau Commercial Association. In presenting the initiative, the president of the association's board, Frederico Ma, recalled that several editions have already been carried out, which managed to “effectively attract a flow of customers” and expand “the clientele to small and medium-sized local stores, encouraging residents to consume in Macau”, injecting “dynamism into the local market”. Frederico Ma assured that the Commercial Association will continue to collaborate proactively with the authorities.

item.title: MIECF led to agreements for more than 60 projects
item.description:
The International Environmental Cooperation Forum and Exhibition resulted in the signing of agreements for more than 60 projects, an increase of 30% compared to the 2025 edition. The 30 thematic activities registered a year-on-year growth of 20% in terms of participants and international visitors, with more than 460 networking sessions being held
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO Over the three days of the International Environmental Cooperation Forum and Exhibition (MIECF), agreements were signed relating to more than 60 projects, which corresponds to an annual increase of more than 30%, according to the organization. In addition, more than 460 networking sessions were held and the 30 thematic activities registered a 20% increase in the participation of exhibitors and international visitors, compared to the 2025 edition. Overall, the four thematic exchange sessions provided more than 300 commercial meetings, related to the acquisition of environmental products, the application of energy efficiency technologies and the construction of green infrastructures. Companies from Mainland China, Portugal, Brazil, Singapore and Thailand, among other countries and regions, participated in these meetings, some of which were classified in the “Fortune Global 500”. Within the scope of industrial integration between the Macao SAR, Portuguese-speaking countries (PLP) and the Deep Cooperation Zone in Hengqin, the four activities of the “One Event, Two Places” initiative were attended by more than 230 traders from PLP and Southeast Asia, as well as representatives from international environmental organizations and green financial institutions. More specifically, a green finance institution in the Macao SAR organized professional forums that managed to attract representatives from more than 180 companies, reinforcing “Macau's role as a bridge in international collaboration on sustainable and low-carbon development”, according to the MIECF. In fact, a Brazilian digital finance company, whose installation in Macau is already underway, presented plans to develop new technologies in the city, in order to boost green cross-border transactions between China and Brazil. Furthermore, a Portuguese technology company achieved “the transformation of innovative research and development technologies into practical applications”, with the purpose of “promoting the implementation of more sustainable ports” and promoting “low-carbon and waste-free cities”, indicates the note. In turn, an association from Guangdong reached preliminary partnership intentions with government entities in the areas of waste treatment and noise monitoring, and also expressed interest in accessing the international markets of Southeast Asia and the PLP, through the Macao SAR. At the same time, a Spanish data information services company established business contacts with environmental protection organizations and local higher education institutions during the green networking fellowship sessions. During “Public Green Day”, 35 companies from Mainland China, Portugal and Brazil showed interest in establishing activities in Macau, which reflects a year-on-year increase of around 60%. According to the organization, 15 groups “have already started or completed the formalities for setting up a company”, in areas related to environmental technologies and the production of ecological products, among others.
“Macao Water” highlighted ecological commitment The Macau Water Supply Society's stand at MIECF featured multiple sustainable elements, such as oriented wood panels, ecological paint and low-energy LED lights, which were used in the stand itself. Materials from previous editions were also reused, aiming to “demonstrate the company’s commitment to environmental protection, recycling and low-carbon practices with zero waste”, according to “Macao Water”. Pointing to the future, the company “will continue to move forward consistently towards fully supporting the sustainable development strategy”, within the scope of its commitments to the three pillars of sustainability: climate, nature and society.
Macau “leads the game” in sustainability “Cartamundi”, a Belgian company that manufactures playing cards, signed a cooperation agreement with the “Asia Pioneer Entertainment” group, with the aim of introducing advanced sustainable production technologies in Macau. The pact was signed by Jason Pearce, managing director of “Cartamundi”, and Herman Ng, executive director of the Asian group. Jason Pearce highlighted Macau's “unique position” as a gateway to the Asian market, and predicted that the partnership will bring global innovation closer to the Macao SAR. Herman Ng said that the technologies and processes that will be introduced in Macau will benefit the local economy and establish new references for sustainable industrial production.

item.title: International parade colored the streets of Macau
item.description:
The Macau International Parade once again brought animation to the streets of Macau, which were filled with people who came to watch the more than 1,600 participating artists. In anticipation of the main parade, several artistic groups collaborated with some gaming operators to organize complementary shows at the resorts
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO The streets of Macau filled up again this Sunday for the Macau International Parade, even despite the cloudy sky. About half an hour late, the first groups started after the mascots “Mak Mak” and “Micaf”, while the rest of the parade waited at the Ruins steps to go down Rua de São Paulo. Until the end of the afternoon, thousands of artists animated the route that crossed Largos de São Domingos and Senado and Avenida Panorâmica do Lago Nam Van, before reaching Praça do Lago Sai Van. Under the theme “The Maritime Silk Road as a bridge for cultural exchange”, more than 60 groups participated in the Parade, including 10 groups from other countries and regions in Asia, Europe and Africa, for an estimated total of 1,600 artists. In anticipation of the main event, several gaming concessionaires collaborated with participating artistic groups to present five shows at the resorts between Thursday and Saturday, according to a statement from the Cultural Institute (IC). In the shows, international artists presented “a variety of performances, including dance, acrobatics, stilts, circus and large-scale mobile installations”, which revealed the unique charm of Macau as a city “that integrates Chinese and Western cultures”, says the IC. SJM presented the “Exotic Dance Gala”, in which Indonesian, Armenian and Mozambican dancers participated, at the Grand Lisboa Palace. Wynn organized the “Enchanting Night View of the Lake”, with traditional dance groups from Armenia and Mozambique and French artists, at the Wynn Palace lake. In turn, MGM Cotai's “Spectacle” hosted the “Land of Marine Wonders”, with images of the ocean, music and dances inspired by the waves and the participation of the “Emoji” group. Sands has prepared a “Time Journey”, in the Venetian's exterior lagoon, with acrobatics, “steampunk” tanks and elements alluding to “modern internet culture”. Finally, Galaxy presented the “Brilliant Parade Fun” at Galaxy Macau, where the French groups “Great Barrier Reef” and “Legendary”, the Indonesian group “Bravery” and the Portuguese “Crassh_Recycled” presented “a dazzling fantasy show”, according to the IC. Furthermore, on Saturday, the Macau International Oriental Dance Association and the “HACHN Dance Ensemble” presented children's dance and traditional Armenian folk dance shows, in the leisure areas of Rotunda de Carlos de Maia and Alameda da Harmonia, in Coloane.

item.title: IL wants special time for exams at EPM and Dili
item.description:
Taking into account the great differences in time zones, the Liberal Initiative urged the Portuguese Government to adapt the examination regime in Portuguese Schools Abroad, namely in Macau and Dili
The Liberal Initiative (IL) presented on Friday a draft resolution that recommends that the Government adapt the regime for national final exams in Portuguese Schools Abroad (EPE), so that students are not harmed by the time difference. The measure takes into account that national final exams take place on the same day and at the same official time as in mainland Portugal, including in EPE. “This rule of absolute simultaneity creates inequality for students located in very distant time zones, namely at the Portuguese School of Macau, where the time difference is eight hours more compared to mainland Portugal, and at the Portuguese School of Díli, in Timor-Leste, where the time difference is nine hours more”, reads the Liberals' explanatory memorandum, recalling that, in these cases, national exams take place in the late afternoon or at night. “Taking highly cognitively demanding tests at times that are out of step with students’ normal biological rhythms can affect performance conditions and raises a question of equity, since students who take the national exam do so at significantly different times of the day”, continues the draft resolution. IL recommends that the Government, as part of the organization of national secondary school final exams, study the creation of exam zones abroad, namely an Asia-Pacific Zone, covering the Escola Portuguesa de Macau and the Escola Portuguesa de Díli, as well as other centers with a time difference equal to or greater than six hours in relation to Lisbon, defining calendars and timetables adapted to the official local time of each exam center”. The objective is that “the tests take place in daytime conditions”. reasonable, preferably from 08:00 and ending by 18:00 local time”. It also recommends that the preparation, by the Institute of Educational Assessment, of equivalent versions of the tests, in terms of difficulty and learning objectives, be allowed for areas where the temporal scheduling justifies it, simultaneously guaranteeing the impossibility of information leakage and fairness between candidates. It advocates that study programs, national correction criteria and the centralization of classification by the National Examinations Jury be fully maintained, ensuring that the national diploma continues to be unique and identical for all students, regardless of where the tests are taken.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Boss demands “improvements” in integration with Hengqin
item.description:
At a meeting held at the Deep Cooperation Zone in Hengqin, the head of the Zone Management Commission and Chief Executive, Sam Hou Fai, stated that “more innovative thinking and a pragmatic approach are needed to better carry out work in Hengqin”. Sam Hou Fai said that the “Two Sessions” approved the general lines of the 15th National Five-Year Plan, “which includes an important plan to better serve Macau's integration into the national development situation, accelerate the construction of the Greater Bay Area and explore the construction of the Deep Cooperation Zone”, according to a statement from the Social Communication Office. Furthermore, he noted that the plan mentions, for the first time, “deepening integrated development between Hengqin and Macau”, “clearly” referring to concrete projects in the areas of transit, industries and population well-being. Reiterating that “this year is crucial for the second phase of development and construction of the Deep Cooperation Zone”, he recalled that the 3rd Five-Year Plan of the MSAR will also be launched and implemented this year, “with integrated development between Hengqin and Macau as a fundamental component”. In this context, the Macao SAR leader stated that it is essential to “pay close attention to shortcomings in current work and proactively implement improvements”. Namely, “fully demonstrate the good character of Hengqin officials and seriously assume the great responsibility of the second phase of construction”, “strengthen investigation and research to form a pragmatic work style”, understand the “concrete needs” of businesses and residents and “implement precise policies”, as well as “maintain integrity in governance and safeguard the fundamental principle of integrity”. The Chief Executive stressed that “the second phase of construction of the Deep Cooperation Zone involves more missions and greater challenges”. In this sense, he said he hopes that “everyone will come together, be proactive, implement the missions assigned by the central authorities, complete the determined plans, plan promising projects and solidly advance the various works”.
Executive wants proposals from the young generation At the inauguration ceremony of the new governing bodies of the Macau Youth Federation, the Chief Executive said he hoped to receive “opinions and proposals from the young generation”. Sam Hou Fai believes that, in the future, “the development space for young people will certainly be wider”. Even so, he stressed that “the growth and progress of youth require determination and persistent efforts”. He thus mentioned three expectations: “cultivate high ambitions”, “act with pragmatism and diligence” and “be bold in innovation”. "The Macao SAR will find unprecedented development opportunities in multiple areas, but it will also be faced with new problems and challenges inherent to this development. I wish everyone to dare to explore and experiment, assuming the role of pioneers", he stated.

item.title: Government calls for stabilization of fuel prices
item.description:
Following the increase in fuel prices in the territory, due to international fluctuations partly caused by tensions in the Middle East, the Macao SAR Government urged the oil sector to stabilize the prices of petroleum products
The Government urged the oil sector to stabilize fuel prices in the territory, following increases internationally, due to tensions in the Middle East. Retail prices in Macau have been affected, said the Economic and Technological Development Services Department (DSEDT), indicating that the Working Group for Fuel Inspection met with the sector to assess the situation and supply. According to Lusa, which cites the most recent Consumer Council (CC) report on fuel prices published on Thursday, premium gasoline at an Esso service station registered an increase of 7.3%, now being sold at 19 patacas per liter, compared to 17.7 patacas in the previous update. At a Shell gas station, the price rose 9.2%, to 18.94 patacas per liter, compared to the previous 17.34 patacas. At the meeting with the sector, DSEDT and the CC said they hope that the sector “will continue to assume its social responsibility, collaborating to stabilize the prices of petroleum products, in order to alleviate the burden on citizens”. Furthermore, they called for “offering different types of benefits to consumers”. Sector representatives stated that, “due to the instability of the international geopolitical situation, the price of crude oil at an international level has registered large fluctuations, with the retail price of fuel in Macau having been adjusted as a result of the increase in international prices”. They also stated that stock and supply are “stable” and have guaranteed “sufficient supply” in the territory. The Working Group assured that it “will continue to closely monitor the variation in the prices of petroleum products”, maintaining communication with the sector. The US and Israel launched a military attack against Iran on February 28 and, in response, Tehran closed the Strait of Hormuz, through which around 20% of the world's oil passes. As a result, oil tanker traffic in the strait fell sharply, increasing supply-related instability and putting pressure on prices.

item.title: Fertility rate reached new historic low
item.description:
Last year, the fertility rate fell again, after being the lowest in the world in 2024. Official data reveal that the Macao SAR ended 2025 with 0.47 births for each woman between 15 and 49 years old, 0.11 less than a year earlier
The fertility rate in Macau fell again in 2025, to a new historic low, after the region recorded the lowest rate in the world in 2024. According to official data released by the Statistics and Census Services Directorate (DSEC), Macau ended last year with 0.47 births for each woman between 15 and 49 years old, 0.11 less than in 2024. Fertility in the territory has been falling for 11 years in a row, to the lowest value since DSEC began calculating this rate in 2001, and far from the value needed to replace generations (2.1). In addition to the drop in fertility, an important indicator for predicting the evolution of the population, Macau also registered 2,871 newborns last year, the lowest number of births in almost 50 years. According to estimates by the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, the region will have the lowest fertility rate in the world in 2024, followed by Singapore, with 0.95 births per woman. The Macau authorities have introduced measures to encourage birth rates, including, in 2025, a subsidy, worth a total of 54 thousand patacas, for children up to three years old. The government leader, Sam Hou Fai, also increased the birth allowance, from 5,418 patacas to 6,500 patacas, and the marriage allowance, from 2,122 patacas to 2,220 patacas. The Executive promised to offer, free of charge, more and better daycare centers and launched a public consultation, which ended on March 16, on the increase in maternity leave in the private sector, from 70 to 90 days. With fewer births, Macau's elderly people aged 65 and over – around 105,200 in 2025 – represented 15.3% of the population, 0.7 percentage points more than in the previous year. The city's population reached 688,900 people at the end of 2025, a slight increase of 0.1% compared to the previous year, with 80,300 being young people up to the age of 14 (11.7%). The elderly population surpassed that of young people for the first time in 2023, with the Macau Government predicting a “super-low birth rate” this decade and close to a quarter of the elderly population by 2041.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Portuguese advisors in Asia promise biweekly meetings with the diaspora
item.description:
The Regional Council of Asia and Oceania (CRAO) of the Council of Portuguese Communities will start holding “fortnightly meetings” with the community and develop “a digital tool” to “connect Portuguese descendants in the diaspora”, as announced in Macau. The CRAO, which concluded its first in-person meeting of the year on Friday, also announced the election of Sara Fernandes, a counselor elected by the Circle of Australia, to the presidency of the regional council for the period 2026-2027, a term that will begin on October 8th. The council did not explain how it will carry out the fortnightly meetings in the various regional circles, namely China, Australia and Timor-Leste, referring new information to “a communiqué”, whose release date was also not announced. Speaking to journalists at the end of the two-day meeting in Macau, the president of CRAO, Rui Marcelo, highlighted the council's recognition of the “diversity and richness” of regional communities, “from Macau, Hong Kong and mainland China, to Australia and East Timor”. “We make it our mission to ensure that your voices are heard with consular entities and institutions of the Republic”, he stated. Asked to explain whether, within the scope of the planned visit of the Chief Executive of Macau to Portugal, Spain and Brussels in April, the CRAO had conveyed to the Portuguese Executive any concerns of the Portuguese diaspora or Portuguese descendants in Macau, namely regarding the issue of residence restrictions imposed on the Portuguese since 2023, Marcelo declined to respond, under the argument that these questions “are channeled” through consulates and embassies to the Ministry of Business Foreigners. “The community brought these concerns to us, it is something that is within the scope of the dialogue that has been taking place and that is under the responsibility of the Consul General of Portugal in Macau and he is the interlocutor for these issues”, he said. “We, as advisors, can make some suggestions, we can convey some concerns, but regarding this issue we cannot overlap”, he added. “We are a consultative body and, as such, we act in this direction”, he also said. A report produced by the Foreign Affairs Committee of the Portuguese Parliament, after a visit to Macau, Hong Kong and Timor-Leste at the end of 2025 and published last January, revealed difficulties raised by the changes – since 2023 – to the regime for granting the Resident Identity Card (BIR) by the Macao SAR, creating increased obstacles to the recruitment of Portuguese workers. He also highlighted significant delays in the processes of granting Portuguese nationality in Macau and in the issuance of passports by the local Registry and Notary Institute, with cases that can take between one and five years. The Portuguese Prime Minister expressed optimism about a solution to the restrictions on Portuguese residence in the Macao SAR, after a meeting he held in September in Macau with Sam Hou Fai. “I believe that things will be on track so that we can have the consecration of a more agile, easier, more expeditious regime and, therefore, that this constraint that we know exists can be overcome”, said Luís Montenegro at the time. The replacement of magistrates, teachers and doctors returning to Portugal was also identified by the parliamentary committee as a problem with a direct impact on the Portuguese community in Macau. These issues should be the subject of analysis at the seventh meeting of the Portugal-Macau Joint Commission, the first since 2019, before the pandemic, scheduled for April in Lisbon.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: 700 exhibitors and 1,500 stands registered for Expo Turismo
item.description:
More than 700 exhibitors from 58 countries or regions, including 13 from Portugal, occupying around 1,500 stands, will participate in the International Tourism (Industry) Expo, which will take place between the 10th and 12th of April at the Venetian. According to the director of Tourism Services, this year's fair “will further strengthen cooperation with Portuguese-speaking countries and make the most of Macau's role as a connecting link”
Under the theme “Global Convergence, Horizons of the Future”, the 14th Macau International Tourism (Industry) Expo, which will take place at the Venetian's Cotai Expo between 10 and 12 April, will feature around 1,500 stands and 700 exhibitors from 58 countries and regions, including 13 companies from Portugal. Furthermore, the exhibition will host 600 national and foreign guest buyers, which demonstrates the event's position as an international business platform and the influence of its brand. During the event, more than 130 thematic activities will be held, including seminars to promote national and international tourism, forums for tour operators and thematic workshops designed specifically for Generation Z, with the aim of “increasing the appeal among younger people”, according to the organization. At the presentation ceremony, the director of Tourism Services (DST), Maria Helena de Senna Fernandes, considered that this year's edition “will further strengthen cooperation with Portuguese-speaking countries and make the most of Macau's role as a liaison”. One of the new highlights of the event is the presence of Pavilions from Portugal and Spain, which, in collaboration with DST and the Institute for Trade and Investment Promotion, will promote tourism products from both countries, and also from China, in a session aimed at companies, enhancing the advantages of Macau as a platform between China and the PLP. The expo will bring the latest tourism information and products from Macau and around the world, including special offers with limited promotional period launched by various exhibiting companies. The public will be able to purchase discounted products, such as hotel accommodation, travel packages and airline tickets. As part of the intelligent experience, the exhibition website will provide a real-time chat service and, for the first time, a matrix of new means of communication with robots was created, which carry out live broadcasts with foreign digital influencers. Hospital das Ilhas participates for the first time with a pavilion dedicated to the presentation of high-level medical equipment. Other ‘big health’ companies from Macau and Hengqin will also be present. In parallel, the University of Tourism, in collaboration with the China Tourism Academy, holds the “High-Level Forum on Tourism Development of the Greater Bay Area”, focused on exploring the emerging opportunities of the silver economy.
V.R.

item.title: Brazil-China cooperation faces obstacles in the environmental field
item.description:
Brazilian economist Jorge Arbache considered that Brazil and China have convergent interests in the energy transition, but warns that standards, certifications and carbon market mechanisms still hinder more effective cooperation. The former vice-president of the Development Bank of Latin America and the Caribbean (CAF) was one of the guest speakers at this year's edition of the Macau International Environmental Cooperation Forum and Exhibition (MIECF). Despite the convergence of interests, the former vice minister and chief economist at the Brazilian Ministry of Planning warns of obstacles that still hinder cooperation, such as standards, certifications and carbon market mechanisms. "If China comes to Brazil with 20 billion US dollars and says 'let's work together', nothing will happen. Before putting the money in, I need to work on these mechanisms", he stated, highlighting the need for regulatory harmonization and reduction of protectionism. In his view, the Brazil-China partnership should not be understood only as a customer-supplier relationship, but as an opportunity for strategic cooperation capable of offering sustainable solutions to the world. “Brazil can be China’s ally in the production of green fuels and low-carbon aluminum, contributing to a faster and more effective global energy transition,” he highlighted. Arbache defended the concept of carbon arbitrage, arguing that countries with clean and cheap energy, like Brazil, can produce energy-intensive goods with much less environmental impact. “You can produce several types of goods, a green chemical product, at a lower cost and within a much shorter timeframe for decarbonization,” he explained. An example cited is green aluminum: in Brazil, the production of one ton emits between two and four tons of carbon dioxide, while in China, dependent on coal, the value rises to 21 to 23 tons. Arbache calculates that transferring all global aluminum production to Brazil could reduce global emissions by up to 4.5%. Cooperation between the two countries has been growing in several areas, with Arbache describing that China already represents between 15% and 18% of electrical energy generation in Brazil, especially in hydroelectricity, and is also a supplier of solar panels and wind turbines that have contributed to increasing the decarbonization of the Brazilian electrical matrix to levels close to 92%. “While China seeks to make many gigantic investments to decarbonize its network, as I said before, Brazil has already made this investment, it already has a decarbonized network,” Arbache told Lusa. Joint projects are also underway in emerging sectors, such as green fuels for navigation and aviation, and initiatives linked to sustainable agribusiness, including the recovery of degraded areas and the reduction of the environmental impact of agricultural production.
Trump is an “unwitting ally” of decarbonization On the other hand, Jorge Arbache stated that the global energy crisis and the policies of North American President Donald Trump are unintentionally accelerating the global energy transition. The economist highlighted that the North American President, by initiating different conflicts related to oil sources, ended up exposing the vulnerability of economies dependent on fossil fuels and, unintentionally, became an involuntary ally of decarbonization. “Trump is making all these moves and what he is achieving with this is showing everyone that everyone has to decarbonize as quickly as possible, because depending on fossils means depending on geopolitical issues of major proportions”, said the former deputy minister and chief economist at the Brazilian Ministry of Planning. The United States and Israel launched a military attack against Iran on February 28 and, in response, Tehran closed the Strait of Hormuz, through which around 20% of the world's oil passes. As a consequence, oil tanker traffic in the strait fell sharply and supply-related instability increased, putting pressure on prices. Arbache explains that the current energy crisis has “uncovered” the structural dependence of oil and gas producing countries, making it clear that decarbonization is not only an environmental issue, but also a strategic one. The need to reduce geopolitical risks is driving energy transition policies in several regions. Arbache argues that the answer involves market-oriented policies, capable of taking advantage of regions with abundant and cheap renewable energy, such as Latin America, to install energy-intensive industries. This logic, which the economist calls ‘powershoring’ would allow not only to reduce production costs, but also to increase the resilience of global value chains. “If you decide to do all your production in Germany, just to give an example, of everything you need, steel, aluminum, chemicals, you will not only produce at a much higher cost, but you will be much more exposed to geopolitical shocks”, he warned. For Arbache, the current energy crisis is an opportunity to rethink the geography of industrial production and accelerate the transition to clean energy. Diversification to areas less exposed to international tensions and with strong renewable capacity is, in the responsible's view, the key to ensuring energy security and economic competitiveness. In the case of China, the economist describes having followed a dual strategy: supporting countries that still need to decarbonize, providing capital and technology, and investing in countries that have already decarbonized, such as Brazil, through partnerships that allow exploring what he calls 'carbon arbitrage'. “You are talking about an agenda with countries that need to decarbonize, another agenda with countries that have already decarbonized, but that will benefit from Chinese investments in the production of things there through ‘carbon arbitration’”, he explained. Regarding Europe, Arbache is critical and considers that the continent maintains an excessively self-centered view, which limits its ability to respond. "Europe seeks a vision of total independence, autonomy from the world. Not only will it not achieve it, I'm talking about energy and also the production of green things, not only will it not achieve it, but what it will achieve will be at a very high cost, period. Because it is structurally uncompetitive", he stated. In the economist's view, the European Union needs to change its mentality and accept that it needs allies in regions less exposed to geopolitical tensions. “Until you change your mindset, you will look at potential allies as competitors, when in fact they are allies,” he added.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Portuguese waste solutions are of interest to Chinese companies
item.description:
A Portuguese business association highlighted in Macau that Chinese environmental companies are interested in Portuguese waste management methods and technologies and see the country as a bridge to markets in Africa and Latin America. According to Rita Barros Silva, from the projects department of the Portuguese Association of Environmental Technology Companies (APEMETA), the Chinese market shows a growing demand for Portuguese management models and technological solutions, mainly “in the area of ​​waste and decarbonization”. In the opposite direction, Portuguese companies look to China with a growing interest in the area of ​​recycling and in the diversification of suppliers of new technological solutions, added the person responsible to Lusa, who participated in this year's edition of the Macau International Environmental Cooperation Forum and Exhibition (MIECF). For the Chinese business sector, the partnership with Portugal is also seen as a strategic asset to overcome communication barriers and access African and South American markets. “Portugal goes further, it goes a lot to the Portuguese-speaking market, whether to the PALOP, or even to Brazil and Latin America and therefore they also look to us for this, as we can be qualified interlocutors to overcome this communication barrier a little”, Barros Silva told Lusa. This triangulation, he highlighted, allows APEMETA to promote national environmental skills while welcoming Chinese investors in Portugal for technical visits that facilitate the completion of business and technical cooperation protocols. Regarding Macau, Rita Barros Silva considered Macau to be a “strategic platform” to consolidate bilateral interest and transform institutional contacts into effective commercial partnerships. More than 50 delegates and companies from Portuguese-speaking countries were present at this year's MIECF. According to the representative, periodic participation in the event in the territory has allowed an essential approach not only to companies in the region, but above all to similar business associations. “This has helped us to introduce Portuguese companies to local Chinese companies and strengthen partnerships with those already in Macau”, he stated.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Psychotherapist arrested on suspicion of sexual abuse and harassment
item.description: The Judiciary Police (PJ) detained a 47-year-old local psychotherapist, who had worked for more than 10 years in a medical institution, on suspicion of sexual abuse and sexual harassment of at least five women, including a patient and four psychology interns. In February 2021, the first victim began receiving treatment at the medical establishment in question, due to emotional problems. During the treatment, the suspect, under the pretext of giving him massages to relieve the pressure, touched his chest, waist and abdominal region, through his underwear. The victim did not resist the harassment because he was panicked. For fear of reprisals, as the psychotherapist was in possession of the victim's personal information, he did not alert the police. However, several years later, the victim was unable to forget the situation and decided to report it. Furthermore, the PJ discovered that, in 2025, the suspect will have repeatedly used excuses to touch the bodies of four other interns. The cases did not lead to complaints because the victims feared that they would fail the internship evaluation. The detainee has already been referred to the Public Prosecutor's Office, accused of sexual abuse of a person incapable of resistance and sexual harassment. Meanwhile, the Macau Association of Certified Psychotherapists highlighted the professional ethics of avoiding physical contact without full consent or that may lead to misunderstandings, urging professionals to encourage trainees to ask for help if they face inappropriate treatment. Furthermore, he promised to work to improve ethical guidelines. The General Women's Association also censured the case, calling on the Government to create a support mechanism.

item.title: Trade in exotic species thrives in China despite animal concerns
item.description:
Pet lovers crowded around a container to take photos of meerkats at a Beijing pet fair, each selling for $320, while nearby, a raccoon paced nervously in a cage barely larger than itself. Crowds of people from across China filled the huge pavilions at the annual pet fair, where exotic animals are more common than cats or dogs. The exotic animal market is growing rapidly, approaching 10 billion yuan, according to Chinese state media. More than 17 million people in China owned exotic pets in 2025, Xinhua reported, and animal rights groups have expressed concern about animal welfare standards. Unconventional pets are particularly popular among young people, with videos on how to raise them widely shared on social media. An 18-year-old man, depositing a deposit to reserve a meerkat, told AFP he was confident it would be easier to raise the animal than his previous cats and dogs. “I feel like raising exotic pets is child’s play in comparison,” said Xiong, who traveled to Beijing from Jiangxi province. He had previously bought a sugar glider – a palm-sized nocturnal possum sold in the same store – and said he found it easier to keep exotic pets as they didn't experience separation anxiety like dogs. It was a “pretty smooth” experience, Mr. Xiong said. “When you want to interact, he’s happy to interact, but when he doesn’t feel like interacting, he’s perfectly happy playing alone,” he said. Yang Xurui took his Argentine green snake to the fair, where he told AFP he was looking for new exotic pets. “I consider her a friend”, confessed the 24-year-old, stroking the crawling creature around his neck. “Every day when I walk in the door, she stands up, tall and straight like a giant chive, to welcome me,” he added. “She keeps me company while I watch TV and, at night, she goes to sleep alone – marking the end of our day together.” Yang said he feels a certain responsibility to dispel the common fear of snakes as pets. “I want to tell everyone that it is not terrible, that it is not something to be afraid of.” China's Ministry of Security has warned of the exotic pet craze, which it says is driven by trend-seekers. “The trade, breeding, medical treatment and abandonment of these exotic animals represent latent safety risks,” he stated in 2025, adding that this requires “serious attention.” However, animal welfare regulations remain lax in China, where pets such as fish, birds and pigs are commonly sold even in shopping malls. The pandemic, in turn, raised fears that animals could carry diseases, including Covid-19, which was believed to have originated in bats.
Lack of knowledge Professor Peter Li, an expert on animal protection policies in China, from the University of Houston-Downtown, said that Chinese authorities should monitor this market, targeting traders, breeders and retailers who depend on the business – and its expansion – to make a profit. Companies involved in the sale and transport of exotic animals allegedly use fraudulent labeling, omit crucial information and resort to deliberate deception to move these animals along supply chains, Professor Li told AFP. Abandoned alien species can reproduce rapidly in the wild, creating significant ecological pressures, while the diseases they carry can pose risks to public health, he added. Public awareness in China about wildlife protection has improved significantly, but some consumers of exotic animals still do not have sufficient knowledge before purchasing these animals, warned the environmental conservation organization WWF (World Wide Fund for Nature). “Some consumers may not be fully aware of which species are legal to keep as pets, whether specific licenses are required, the different levels of difficulty of care for each species and the long-term financial costs,” he added. At the fair, 26-year-old Zhang Yue agreed that taking certain animals “into environments inhabited by humans can have various consequences.” Despite this, Zhang said she would still consider getting a meerkat as they are "absolutely adorable."
JTM with Xinhua and international agencies
